Bryn Gallagher Letsch of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Billy James Smith

RULING:
DEFENDANT FRASER'S BOILER SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Specifically, Mr. Coker's deposition testimony precludes defendant from demonstrating that any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred after the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, Mr. Coker's declaration creates a triable issue whether any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred before the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos. Finally, even if there was no evidence that any alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant occurred before the time plaintiff learned of the hazards of asbestos, the sophisticated user defense would not be a complete bar to plaintiff's claims against defendant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Cathy Meyer of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #11596 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
